scope: Home page and Solutions page media planning before Sanity draft seeding.
doNotDo: Do not edit approved content maps.
Do not remove or replace existing Sanity image refs.
Do not use real client or personal data.

transferWorkflow.safetyRule: Codefy OS never writes to Sanity. It only prepares media outputs.
steps: Capture screens, tune demo states, or recreate faithful mockups inside Codefy OS using demo-safe data only.
Export approved image files from Codefy OS into marketing-media/tranzkit/.
Keep content/planning/ in the website repo as the mapping and staging-plan source of truth.
Let tanzkit_website_v2 own Sanity upload, image ref mapping, draft seeding, and publishing.

statusDefinitions.not_ready: Asset is still being prepared in Codefy OS.
statusDefinitions.ready_for_transfer: Asset export is approved and ready to move into the website repo.
statusDefinitions.transferred_to_website_repo: Asset file has been copied into the website repo staging folder.
statusDefinitions.uploaded_to_sanity: Website repo owners uploaded the staged asset into Sanity.
statusDefinitions.mapped_to_content: Website repo owners mapped the resulting Sanity asset ref into website content structures.
statusDefinitions.approved: Asset is fully approved after upload and content mapping.

homepageFeatureTabs.renderingBehavior: Uses next/image with width 1200, height 800, object-contain, no forced crop.

homepageScreenshotCarousel.renderingBehavior: Uses next/image with width 1200, height 800, object-contain inside cards.

homepageSeoOg.renderingBehavior: Home metadata explicitly transforms ogImage to 1200x630.

solutionsSeoOg.renderingBehavior: Solutions metadata currently does not pass ogImage into generateResourceMetadata.
solutionsSeoOg.implementationRisk: The asset can be prepared now, but it will not render in metadata until the page passes solutionsPage.seo?.ogImage.

featureAndCarouselImages.basis: Exact dimensions are inferred from homepage feature tab and screenshot carousel components.

ogImages.basis: Exact OG dimensions are enforced by Home metadata and are standard for social preview images.

notes: Figma file structure is created and verified. Use this file as the single production planning source for website media.
Use a single neutral design system layer for labels, browser chrome, AI insight cards, and soft callout chips.
Keep screen data demo-safe and remove any tenant, user, GPS token, or billing identifiers.
For recreated mockups, use real Tranzkit screen structure as source truth and simplify only for clarity, not invention.
aiIntegratedVisualLanguage: Use embedded AI insight cards inside the operating workflow, not disconnected chatbot bubbles.
Prefer blue and green Tranzkit styling with clean neutral surfaces and restrained accent states.
Use dashboards, mobile frames, analytics cards, attendance validation cards, and finance report cards as the repeating language.
Keep labels minimal, neutral, and product-like rather than promotional.
Do not imply unsupported autonomy or unsupported AI approvals.
animatedSequenceGuidance: Build animated sequences as ordered Figma frame sets first, not as plugin-first exports.
Prefer PNG sequence export from Figma and later GIF assembly by script as the primary automated path.
Keep motion restrained and functional: state changes, panel reveals, timeline progression, and controlled review actions.
Do not animate unsupported product behavior or imply autonomous approvals.

aiIntegratedOsVisualStrategy.strategyStatement: Every media asset should make Tranzkit feel like a fully AI integrated operating system for employee transportation, where AI appears as embedded workflow intelligence across planning, operations, attendance, analytics, and finance.
aiIntegratedOsVisualStrategy.primaryVisualNarrative: Show Tranzkit as one connected operating layer spanning enterprise control, operator execution, field tracking, attendance validation, safety analytics, and executive reporting, with AI insight cards embedded in the exact workflow where decisions are made.
proofPointVisuals: AI agent panel or assistant card inside operations workflow
AI assisted app navigation or command panel
Smart employee attendance with geofence and biometric validation
Smart driver attendance and automatic trip execution
Background tracking and battery aware tracking
Trip playback and planned vs actual comparison
screensOrMockupsNeeded: Enterprise planning with AI insight card
Operator execution with AI validation card
Finance visibility with AI-generated executive report card
Driver mobile workflow with automatic trip lifecycle and tracking cues
Trip analysis with playback, safety analytics, and planned-vs-actual comparison
Attendance validation surfaces for employees and drivers
Enterprise service validation and rider trip visibility surfaces
Fleet readiness and exception insight surfaces

whereEachVisualShouldAppear.homeFeatureEnterprise: Enterprise control and AI-assisted planning story
whereEachVisualShouldAppear.homeFeatureOperator: Operator execution and AI-assisted validation story
whereEachVisualShouldAppear.homeCarouselPlanning: Real product proof of planning depth with light AI framing
whereEachVisualShouldAppear.homeCarouselOperator: Real product proof of operator control with light AI framing
whereEachVisualShouldAppear.homeCarouselFinance: AI-assisted finance and executive reporting story
whereEachVisualShouldAppear.homeCarouselDriverField: Driver workflow, automatic trip execution, and tracking story
whereEachVisualShouldAppear.homeOptionalFleetReadiness: Readiness, maintenance, and exception monitoring story
whereEachVisualShouldAppear.homeOptionalLiveTracking: Trip playback and comprehensive trip analytics story
whereEachVisualShouldAppear.homeOg: Language-neutral summary of the AI integrated transport OS
whereEachVisualShouldAppear.solutionsEnterprisesPassengersAttendance: Smart employee attendance, work-location validation, and biometric proof story
whereEachVisualShouldAppear.solutionsEnterprisesPassengersServiceVal: Enterprise service validation and planned-vs-actual control story
whereEachVisualShouldAppear.solutionsEnterprisesPassengersRiderVisib: Passenger trip clarity, live status, and service-confidence story
whereEachVisualShouldAppear.solutionsEnterprisesPassengersEnterprise: Enterprise AI-assisted executive, financial, and controlled-update reporting story

risks: A generic dashboard look weakens the new AI integrated OS positioning.
Overstating AI autonomy can create misleading product claims.
Sparse tenant data can make real screenshots look less credible than faithful rebuilds.
Too much embedded copy inside visuals can break the shared-language-neutral media policy.
Trip analytics or map-heavy captures can expose sensitive locations, ids, or query strings if not carefully cropped.
doNotShowRules: Do not show AI as a detached floating chatbot with no workflow context.
Do not show unsupported feature claims or invented product modules.
Do not imply that AI replaces human approval.
Do not imply that AI autonomously dispatches trips or approves financial changes.
Do not use unsafe driving judgement language or punitive framing.
No real tenant names except approved Zamalek demo context if visually safe.
No raw trip ID in visible crop.
No claim that AI replaces human approval.
No claim that AI autonomously dispatches or approves financial changes.

animatedSequencePlan.localeSpecificRule: Create EN and AR variants only if embedded text is required.
blockers: Confirm tanzkit_website_v2 can render animated GIF from Sanity image fields without freezing animation.
Approve static media assets first before any animated derivative export starts.
Keep GIF production blocked until target page content maps are seeded and reviewed in the website repo.
manualPluginFallbackSteps: Open TinyImage Compressor or GIF Export plugin.

scriptBasedAlternative.approach: Export ordered PNG frames from Figma, then assemble the GIF locally with a script when requested.
scriptBasedAlternative.implementationNote: Do not implement the script yet unless asked.

sourceFramesNeeded: AI agent panel opens inside workflow
AI surfaces an exception or insight
AI prepares operational or financial report card
sequences.notes: High-value hero sequence for later Home or Solutions use if website rendering supports GIF cleanly. Keep AI embedded in the operational workflow rather than as a detached assistant.

sourceFramesNeeded: harsh braking and harsh acceleration events
sequences.notes: Use Trip Analysis as the truth source. Keep map detail safe, avoid raw query strings, and make the sequence about analytics rather than generic tracking.
Attendance proof sequence for future page scope. Use neutral UI cards, geofence indicators, and validation states without large embedded copy.

sourceFramesNeeded: background tracking and battery aware tracking
sequences.notes: Show driver execution without distracting the driver. Emphasize automatic lifecycle transitions and background-safe tracking behavior, not manual interaction density.

approvalChecklistTemplate: Demo-safe data only; no real client, employee, driver, vehicle plate, phone, invoice, payment, or GPS data.
No browser URL bar, query token, dev toolbar, debug panel, or transient toast is visible unless intentionally mocked.
English and Arabic text remains legible if shown inside the capture.
Primary KPI, chart, or table focal area remains readable at target export size.
No existing Sanity asset ref is changed until a separate approved upload and seeding step.
Authenticity label must be either Captured from product or Marketing composite; never Concept visual.
If the asset is recreated in Figma, every visible module, entity, and workflow must map back to a real Tranzkit capability.

assets.screenshotComposition: Capture a planning-heavy calendar or schedule view with visible filters, planned-vs-actual context, and route density. Keep the right or top region clean enough for a short overlay label if Figma framing is added later.
assets.aiStoryRole: Enterprise AI operating-system proof: planning, attendance validation, operator performance, finance, and embedded AI insight in one control surface.
assets.aiVisualDirection: Enterprise control mockup with planning, attendance validation, operator performance, finance, and an embedded AI agent insight card placed inside the workflow rather than as a detached chat bubble.
assets.figmaFrameStyle: Single desktop product frame with subtle browser chrome, soft shadow, and one planning KPI strip.

alt.en: Enterprise planning dashboard showing schedules, demand, and service control.
alt.ar: لوحة تخطيط للشركة تعرض الجداول والطلب والتحكم في الخدمة.

approvalChecklist: Use demo schedule, route, and supplier names only.
Keep calendar counts and validation indicators readable.
Avoid exposing employee names or exact trip identifiers.
assets.sourceTruth: Faithful marketing recreation of real Tranzkit enterprise planning workflows based on schedule_calendar_screen and client_booking_dashboard patterns already present in the product.
assets.assetMethodNote: Use a recreated mockup because the feature image slot must support the AI integrated OS story. Keep routes, schedules, bookings, attendance validation, performance, pricing visibility, service control, and the embedded AI insight card aligned with real product capabilities.

assets.screenshotComposition: Prefer the Schedule Assignments tab with visible driver, vehicle, supplier, working-day, and validation context. If QR context is stronger, use a clean operator-focused composite from the same screen family.
assets.aiStoryRole: Operator AI operating-system proof: assignments, live exceptions, trip analytics, readiness, and AI-assisted validation inside execution flow.
assets.aiVisualDirection: Operator execution mockup with assignments, live exceptions, trip analytics, driver readiness, and an embedded AI agent validation card tied to the operating workflow.
assets.figmaFrameStyle: Desktop product frame with crisp table density, a highlighted assignment row, and restrained callout tags.

alt.en: Operator execution workspace showing schedule assignments, drivers, vehicles, and field status.
alt.ar: مساحة تشغيل للمشغل تعرض التعيينات والسائقين والمركبات وحالة التنفيذ الميداني.

approvalChecklist: Show operator-side execution evidence, not generic admin tables.
Keep assignment validation or status signals visible.
Remove any real driver names, phone numbers, or vehicle plates.
assets.sourceTruth: Faithful marketing recreation of real Tranzkit operator execution workflows based on driver_assignments_screen, live_trips_screen, and related assignment flows in the repo.
assets.assetMethodNote: Use a recreated mockup because the feature slot needs a clean operator story with assignments, trips, drivers, vehicles, exceptions, analytics, and an embedded AI validation layer inside the workflow.

assets.navigationPath: My Workspaces -> Sales Management -> Routes & Schedules -> Schedules
assets.screenshotComposition: Use the Schedules view with pricing columns and assigned resource columns visible so planning, pricing, and readiness read in one frame. Keep the schedule table dominant and crop out weak surrounding chrome.
assets.aiStoryRole: Real proof of planning depth inside the AI integrated OS narrative.
assets.aiVisualDirection: Capture planning or schedules view, then optionally frame it later with a small AI insight overlay in Figma. Keep the overlay lightweight and subordinate to the real product screenshot.
assets.figmaFrameStyle: Full-width desktop dashboard frame with clean edge crop and no extra annotations.

alt.en: Enterprise planning dashboard with bookings, utilization, and service setup.
alt.ar: لوحة تخطيط للشركة تعرض الحجوزات والاستغلال وتجهيز الخدمة.

approvalChecklist: Pricing, route, client, and assigned resource columns must be visible at first glance.
Do not expose unsafe personal names or sensitive destination details.
Keep the Schedules state readable without over-cropping the table.
assets.sourceTruth: Real Tranzkit routes_screen_v3 Schedules view in the Sales Management workspace, using demo-safe schedule, pricing, and assignment data with tuned table state.
assets.assetMethodNote: Use a real screenshot and tune the Schedules table state first. If later framing is needed, add only a lightweight AI insight overlay without turning the asset into a synthetic dashboard.
assets.productionNotes: Wave 1 review asset generated as a 1200x800 PNG from the approved Schedules raw capture. The crop is left-weighted to keep schedule, route, client, and timing context while reducing the personal-resource columns.
assets.privacyNotes: The review crop removes browser/query chrome and minimizes visible resource-link columns. Remaining labels are from the user-approved demo session; if stricter anonymization is needed later, this asset should move to Figma rebuild.
assets.captureNotes: Raw cropped schedules capture saved from Sales Management -> Routes & Schedules -> Schedules. Crop removes top account chrome and empty lower area; suitable as tuned screenshot source.

assets.navigationPath: My Workspaces -> Daily Operations -> Links and Assignments
assets.screenshotComposition: Capture the operator control state through links, assignments, and execution-readiness context. Keep the primary assignment area visible and avoid empty or ambiguous sub-states.
assets.aiStoryRole: Real proof of operator control with AI-assisted validation and exception awareness.
assets.aiVisualDirection: Capture assignments or operations control view. Later Figma framing may add a lightweight AI validation or exception summary card without overpowering the real screen.
assets.figmaFrameStyle: Desktop operations frame with visible assignment rows, status chips, and restrained surrounding chrome.

alt.en: Operator control view showing assignments, linked resources, and execution readiness.
alt.ar: واجهة تحكم للمشغل تعرض التعيينات والموارد المرتبطة وجاهزية التنفيذ.

approvalChecklist: Assignment rows or resource-link state must be readable at first glance.
Use demo-safe operator, driver, vehicle, and supplier labels only.
Avoid empty states, stale zero-state widgets, or debug-only surfaces.
assets.sourceTruth: Faithful marketing recreation grounded in the verified Daily Operations Links and Assignments workflow, using demo-safe operator execution concepts without exposing raw operator PII.
assets.assetMethodNote: This asset moved to Figma rebuild because the verified Daily Operations route exposed phone numbers and PII-heavy rows. Rebuild it from the verified workflow with embedded AI validation cues and no raw personal data.
assets.productionNotes: Wave 1 review asset generated in Figma as a shared-language-neutral marketing composite. It shows assignments, linked resources, readiness, service state, and a small embedded AI validation card without any raw personal rows.
assets.privacyNotes: This mockup intentionally avoids phone numbers, personal names, and PII-heavy assignment rows that were present in the rejected raw route state.
assets.captureNotes: Daily Operations -> Links and Assignments reached successfully, but the Associations state exposed phone numbers and PII-heavy rows. The attempted tuned screenshot was rejected and this asset now moves to Figma rebuild.

assets.navigationPath: My Workspaces -> Supplier Management -> Supplier Balance
assets.screenshotComposition: Use Supplier Balance or Invoices as the truth source, but rebuild the final visual around payable status, balances, statement status, and payment timing because the current tenant data is too sparse for a launch-ready raw screenshot.
assets.aiStoryRole: AI-integrated finance proof: supplier balance, invoices, settlements, and AI-generated executive report visibility.
assets.aiVisualDirection: Finance visibility mockup showing supplier balance, invoices, payables, settlement context, and an AI-generated financial or executive report card using safe demo values only.
assets.figmaFrameStyle: Desktop finance frame with clear payable summary cards, statement status chips, and one curated finance table segment.

alt.en: Finance dashboard showing receivables, payables, cash flow, and profitability visibility.
alt.ar: لوحة مالية تعرض الذمم المدينة والدائنة والتدفق النقدي ورؤية الربحية.

approvalChecklist: No real invoice numbers, balances, bank data, payment references, or supplier private details.
Financial metric labels must remain legible at 1200x800.
Use only verified product concepts: payables, balances, statements, payments, and visibility status.
assets.sourceTruth: Faithful marketing recreation grounded in the verified Supplier Management finance views, especially supplier_balance_screen and supplier payment flows, because the current tenant routes are accessible but visually too sparse for a website-ready tuned screenshot.
assets.assetMethodNote: Change this to a Figma recreated mockup for the first release. The verified tenant finance routes are real and useful as truth sources, but the current Supplier Balance, Supplier Payments, and Sales Invoices states are too empty to carry a strong tuned screenshot.
assets.productionNotes: Wave 1 review asset generated in Figma as a finance-visibility marketing composite. It shows supplier balances, invoices, payables, settlement timing, and an AI executive report card using safe rounded demo values only.
assets.privacyNotes: This mockup uses generic supplier names and demo-safe rounded values. It does not expose real invoice references, payment details, bank data, or sensitive finance records.

assets.screenshotComposition: Show a live operations table or tracking-focused state with obvious trip status, route context, and field action affordances. If a Figma montage is used, keep desktop as the base and add one mobile-sized inset only.
assets.aiStoryRole: Driver workflow and intelligent automation proof inside the AI integrated OS story.
assets.aiVisualDirection: Driver mobile workflow mockup showing automatic trip start and end, background tracking, battery-aware tracking, and trip status without driver distraction.
assets.figmaFrameStyle: Desktop-first product frame with optional mobile inset and compact route-status chips.

alt.en: Field operations view showing live trips, driver activity, and QR-supported workflows.
alt.ar: واجهة عمليات ميدانية تعرض الرحلات الحية ونشاط السائقين وسير العمل المدعوم برموز QR.

approvalChecklist: No live device token, trip share token, or exact public URL can appear in the capture.
Trip status and field action context should be visible in the first glance.
If a mobile inset is added, it must stay readable without overpowering the base frame.
assets.sourceTruth: Faithful marketing recreation grounded in real Tranzkit live_trips_screen, live-tracking flows, QR-supported attendance concepts, and driver workflow capabilities in the codebase.
assets.assetMethodNote: Prefer a recreated mockup for the first release. The visual should emphasize automatic trip execution, background tracking, and intelligent field workflow cues while staying faithful to real capabilities.

recommendedAppScreen.screenName: Composite from enterprise, operations, and finance captures
recommendedAppScreen.suggestedRouteOrViewName: Composite build; no single app route

assets.screenshotComposition: Build a wide social card with one dominant product shot and two secondary cropped panels representing planning and finance. Leave safe text space for brand, promise, and subtle gradient treatment.
assets.aiStoryRole: Top-level AI integrated OS positioning card for the homepage.
assets.aiVisualDirection: Language-neutral branded OG card showing enterprise control, operator execution, trip analytics, and finance visibility as one AI integrated transport OS. Avoid large embedded English or Arabic text.
assets.figmaFrameStyle: Wide OG card with layered screenshot crops, restrained branding, and no tiny unreadable UI fragments.

alt.en: Tranzkit home social card showing planning, operations, and finance visibility.
alt.ar: بطاقة مشاركة للصفحة الرئيسية تعرض التخطيط والتشغيل والرؤية المالية في ترانزكيت.

approvalChecklist: Home OG must read clearly at thumbnail size.
Use only demo-safe screenshot fragments.
Keep messaging aligned with the approved homepage promise.
assets.sourceTruth: Composite card built from real Tranzkit planning, operations, and finance workflows and the approved homepage messaging scope.
assets.assetMethodNote: Design this as a Figma social card for launch. It should summarize the AI integrated operating system narrative without introducing unsupported features or oversized text.

recommendedAppScreen.screenName: Composite from enterprise-control and operator-execution captures
recommendedAppScreen.suggestedRouteOrViewName: Composite build; no single app route

assets.screenshotComposition: Use a split-composition card: enterprise planning on one side, operator execution on the other, with brand lockup centered or offset. Keep both sides visually balanced.
assets.aiStoryRole: Top-level enterprise-to-operator AI connection card for Solutions.
assets.aiVisualDirection: Language-neutral branded OG card showing enterprise control and operator execution connected by AI agents.
assets.figmaFrameStyle: Wide split-screen OG frame with two screenshot panels and restrained connector accents.

alt.en: Tranzkit solutions social card showing enterprise control and operator execution.
alt.ar: بطاقة مشاركة لصفحة الحلول تعرض تحكم الشركة وتنفيذ المشغل.

approvalChecklist: The visual must communicate two connected solution sides at thumbnail size.
Do not include route tokens, share tokens, or real trip identifiers.
Remember the current Solutions metadata code still needs ogImage wiring before use.
assets.sourceTruth: Composite card built from real Tranzkit enterprise-control and operator-execution workflows and the approved Solutions messaging scope.
assets.assetMethodNote: Design this as a Figma social card for launch. It should visually connect enterprise control and operator execution through embedded AI assistance without inventing a new product surface.

assets.navigationPath: My Workspaces -> test -> Owned Fleet -> Fleet / Maintenance / Accident Reports
assets.screenshotComposition: Use Owned Fleet as the truth source and rebuild a cleaner readiness composition around vehicle status, maintenance, and incidents because the current tenant view only exposes a very small fleet state.
assets.aiStoryRole: Fleet readiness and exception-intelligence proof for the AI integrated OS narrative.
assets.aiVisualDirection: Fleet readiness mockup showing vehicle readiness, maintenance, accident follow-up, driver readiness, and AI exception insight.
assets.figmaFrameStyle: Desktop fleet-readiness frame with a focused vehicle summary, readiness statuses, and maintenance/incident callouts.

alt.en: Fleet readiness view showing maintenance tasks, vehicle status, and accident follow-up.
alt.ar: واجهة جاهزية الأسطول تعرض مهام الصيانة وحالة المركبات ومتابعة الحوادث.

approvalChecklist: This remains optional until the homepage content map adds an approved slot.
Do not expose real vehicle plates, driver names, phone numbers, or compliance identifiers.
Keep readiness, maintenance, and incident cues immediately visible.
assets.sourceTruth: Faithful marketing recreation grounded in the verified Owned Fleet workspace route and its Fleet, Maintenance, and Accident Reports tabs, because the current tenant demo state is too thin for a strong tuned screenshot.
assets.assetMethodNote: Change this to a Figma recreated mockup for the first release. The route is verified and truthful, but the current tenant fleet state is too small and sparse to produce a high-quality AI-assisted readiness visual.

assets.navigationPath: Daily Operations -> Trips or Trips Live Attendance -> Trip Analysis
assets.screenshotComposition: Use Trip Analysis as the approved live-tracking-like surface and capture the map, trip timeline, and telemetry-rich context while cropping out query strings and any sensitive exact location details if they appear.
assets.aiStoryRole: Trip playback and comprehensive trip analytics proof inside the AI integrated OS narrative.
assets.aiVisualDirection: Trip analysis screen showing playback and trip analytics, with emphasis on planned vs actual, speed behavior, phone usage, harsh braking, and harsh acceleration when visible and safe.
assets.figmaFrameStyle: Tracking-focused desktop analytics frame with map, trip timeline, and compact telemetry panels.

alt.en: Live tracking view showing trip status, route context, and current vehicle tracking.
alt.ar: واجهة تتبع حي تعرض حالة الرحلة وسياق المسار وتتبع المركبة الحالي.

approvalChecklist: This remains optional until the homepage content map adds an approved slot.
No device token, share URL, query string, or exact private location data may be visible in the final crop.
Use the approved trip-analysis trip only, or another explicitly approved demo-safe trip-analysis URL later.
assets.sourceTruth: Real Tranzkit trip_analysis_screen route used as the best available live-tracking-style surface for this tenant, grounded in completed trip telemetry and trip analysis data.
assets.assetMethodNote: Keep this as a tuned screenshot, but use trip analysis as the approved live-tracking-like proof surface. The final crop must avoid query strings, raw trip ids, and exact sensitive map details.
assets.productionNotes: Wave 1 review asset generated as a 1200x800 PNG from the approved Trip Analysis capture. The review crop keeps the map and analytics together with no browser chrome or query state.
assets.privacyNotes: The crop removes the identifying trip header, query string, and raw trip ID. The map remains generalized at website size and does not expose exact private addresses, though it still reflects a broad real route area.
assets.captureNotes: Replaced the earlier crop with a new capture taken from the current live Trip Analysis view. This version keeps the map and analytics together, removes the identifying trip header, and trims the right and bottom whitespace.

assets.navigationPath: Daily Operations -> Trips or Trips Live Attendance -> Trip Analysis
assets.screenshotComposition: Use a safe below-header crop from Trip Analysis that keeps the map and analytics together while emphasizing trip health, planned vs actual, and driver-behavior metrics without exposing identifying header data or precise route details.
assets.recommendedVisual: Trip analytics panel showing playback-oriented review, planned vs actual variance, speed behavior, phone usage, and harsh-event analysis.
assets.recommendedPurpose: Support the Operators and Drivers page with concrete proof of comprehensive trip analytics rather than generic live tracking.
assets.aiStoryRole: Trip analytics and operational review proof for the Operators and Drivers narrative.
assets.aiVisualDirection: Use the analytics panel as product-authentic proof of playback, planned vs actual comparison, speed behavior, phone usage, harsh braking, and harsh acceleration.
assets.figmaFrameStyle: Analytics-focused crop that can later be framed inside a wider neutral product card if needed.

alt.en: Trip analytics view showing safety score, planned versus actual comparison, and driver behavior insights.
alt.ar: واجهة تحليلات رحلة تعرض درجة السلامة ومقارنة المخطط بالفعلي ومؤشرات سلوك السائق.

approvalChecklist: Do not show trip ids, query strings, driver names, vehicle plates, or exact route map details.
Keep planned versus actual, speed, and phone-usage proof points readable.
Move to Figma rebuild only if a wider and cleaner marketing composition is later required.
assets.sourceTruth: Real Tranzkit trip_analysis_screen capture from the approved trip analysis route, cropped to a safe below-header frame that keeps the map and analytics together while avoiding identifying header data and exact route details.
assets.assetMethodNote: Start with a tuned screenshot from Trip Analysis. Rebuild in Figma only if a broader marketing composition is needed after review.
assets.productionNotes: Wave 1 review asset generated as a 1200x800 PNG from the approved Trip Analysis raw capture. The crop preserves the map plus analytics story and stays product-authentic.
assets.privacyNotes: The review crop removes the identifying trip header and query state. It avoids exact address-level exposure, though broad route geography remains visually inferable from the map at low detail.
assets.captureNotes: Replaced the earlier crop with a new capture taken from the current live Trip Analysis view. This version keeps the map and analytics together, removes the identifying trip header, and trims the right and bottom whitespace.

assets.aiStoryRole: Embedded AI workflow proof for Operators and Drivers.
assets.aiVisualDirection: Show AI agents inside the operating workflow, not as a detached chatbot. Include schedule validation, assignment gap review, trip analytics review, operational and financial reports, and controlled update review.

approvalChecklist: Keep AI embedded inside real workflow surfaces, not as a detached chatbot bubble.
Do not claim autonomous dispatch or autonomous financial approval.
Use safe demo labels and product-authentic operational entities only.

assets.recommendedVisual: Attendance validation dashboard showing employee transport eligibility, work location validation, geofence status, biometric confirmation, and enterprise-ready audit context.
assets.recommendedPurpose: Support the Enterprises and Passengers page with clear proof of smart employee attendance governance tied to transport operations.

assets.aiVisualDirection: Show smart employee attendance validation inside a real enterprise workflow, with any AI insight card embedded as a secondary review aid rather than a detached assistant.

Keep AI cues embedded in the validation workflow and avoid autonomy claims.

assets.screenshotComposition: Show one enterprise reporting surface that connects attendance evidence, service validation, operator performance, finance review, and a controlled AI-assisted update panel.
assets.recommendedVisual: Executive and financial reporting view with AI-assisted summaries, operator performance proof, planned-vs-actual review, and controlled update review.
assets.recommendedPurpose: Support the Enterprises and Passengers page with credible AI-assisted executive and financial visibility inside the operating workflow.
assets.aiStoryRole: Enterprise executive intelligence and controlled-update proof.
assets.aiVisualDirection: Show AI reporting inside a real enterprise workflow with reporting cards, finance visibility, attendance evidence, and controlled update review rather than a detached chatbot.
assets.figmaFrameStyle: Desktop executive-reporting frame with summary cards, one curated report table segment, and embedded review cards.

alt.en: Enterprise reporting dashboard showing attendance, service, finance, and AI-assisted executive summaries.

approvalChecklist: Keep reporting, service, and finance proof points legible at 1200x800.
Do not expose sensitive finance values, bank details, private invoice references, or personal contact data.
Do not imply AI approves financial changes autonomously.
assets.sourceTruth: Faithful marketing recreation grounded in approved Tranzkit executive reporting, operator performance review, finance visibility, and controlled-update concepts already represented in the approved page story.
assets.assetMethodNote: Use a Figma recreated mockup by default because the page needs a high-signal enterprise reporting composition that stays safe, language-neutral, and visually stronger than a sparse raw finance capture.

assets.targetAppRoute: Reference composition from rider trip visibility, assigned trip timing, live status, and structured feedback states

assets.recommendedPurpose: Support the page with a passenger-side proof point that makes commute visibility and service confidence tangible.
assets.aiStoryRole: Passenger trip clarity and service-confidence proof within the same operating system.
assets.aiVisualDirection: Keep the rider visual clean, mobile-aware, and operational. If AI appears at all, it should be a light insight or service-status aid embedded in the workflow, not a chatbot layer.

assets.aiStoryRole: Service-validation and operator-accountability proof for the AI integrated OS story.

approvalChecklist: Keep service validation, planned-vs-actual comparison, and operator accountability visible at first glance.
Do not expose phone numbers, emails, exact addresses, raw trip ids, query strings, or sensitive map details.
Stay faithful to supported Tranzkit delivery, review, and follow-up workflows.
assets.sourceTruth: Faithful marketing recreation grounded in approved Tranzkit service validation, operator delivery review, rider visibility, and planned-vs-actual workflows described in the approved Enterprises and Passengers page.
assets.assetMethodNote: Use a Figma recreated mockup by default because the page needs a high-signal validation visual and no verified safe service-review capture has been approved as strong enough yet.
assets.productionNotes: Wave 1 review asset generated in Figma as a shared-language-neutral marketing composite. It shows operator delivery, live status, planned-versus-actual review, rider impact, and evidence-backed follow-up in one review surface.
assets.privacyNotes: This mockup uses service-line labels and aggregate rider counts only. It does not expose personal rows, phone numbers, exact addresses, query strings, or raw trip identifiers.

safetyRisks: App captures can leak real tenant, client, employee, driver, supplier, and vehicle data if screenshots are taken from a live environment.
Live trips and live tracking surfaces can leak device tokens, share tokens, trip IDs, and exact location data if the URL or tracking UI is left visible.
Finance surfaces can leak real invoice numbers, balances, payment references, or bank-related details if demo data is not used.
Solutions page OG support is not fully wired in the current frontend metadata path, so asset creation is safe but usage is blocked by implementation.
The Figma planning file is ready, and any raw capture should stay limited to the already approved Zamalek browser states or later user-approved replacements.
inputsNeededFromUser: The approval owner name for approvedBy when assets move beyond planned status.
Confirmation of the final local export destination if it should differ from content/planning/exports/.
Any replacement approved trip-analysis URL if the current demo-safe trip becomes unavailable later.

assetCreationReadiness.reason: Planning is ready, but final asset production remains intentionally paused until the user starts the next approved capture or Figma production wave.

approvedCapturePlan.aiRole: Enterprise control with embedded AI insight
captureStateInstructions: Open Schedule Calendar with a dense but readable planning state showing routes, schedules, service requirements, and demand context.
Use approved Zamalek tenant data only after the user visually approves the browser state.
Capture the raw screen as a design reference only; do not export the raw capture as the final asset.
figmaDesignInstructions: Build a polished enterprise planning composition faithful to real Tranzkit planning workflows.
Show planning, attendance validation, operator performance, finance, and an embedded AI agent insight card inside the operating workflow.
Avoid embedded marketing text and keep any visible text product-UI related only.

approvedCapturePlan.targetAppRoute: Tranzkit workspace -> driver_assignments_screen -> Schedule Assignments / QR context

captureStateInstructions: Open Fleet & Schedule Management with operator-relevant assignments, readiness, and execution context visible.
Prefer the Schedule Assignments tab and capture QR or field execution references only if the user approves that state.
Capture raw reference screens only; the final asset will be rebuilt in Figma.
figmaDesignInstructions: Create a marketing-clean operator execution composition using assignments, trips, drivers, vehicles, statuses, and QR-aware field context.
Include live exceptions, trip analytics, driver readiness, and an embedded AI agent validation card inside the workflow.
Stay faithful to real Tranzkit operator capabilities and do not invent unsupported mobile or control features.
Keep table density readable and highlight one strong operator workflow.

approvedCapturePlan.navigationPath: My Workspaces -> Sales Management -> Routes & Schedules -> Schedules
approvedCapturePlan.aiRole: Real planning proof with lightweight AI framing
captureStateInstructions: Open Sales Management from My Workspaces, then open Routes & Schedules and switch the inner tab to Schedules.
Use the resulting workspace URL with workspace=6488937e-d4a3-409e-b379-012c0374b877 and routes_entity=schedules plus routes_tab=schedules if the state needs to be restored.
Tune visible columns and row density so route, client, pricing, and assigned resource context all read well at website size.
Capture the planning or schedules view, then later frame it with a small AI insight overlay in Figma if appropriate without overdoing the overlay.
If the state is weak or sparse after tuning, mark the asset as needsFigmaRebuild instead of forcing a bad screenshot.
figmaDesignInstructions: Use only light framing cleanup if the screenshot needs browser chrome or crop polish.
Do not materially reconstruct the screen unless the tuned screenshot fails review.
Keep the final asset visually product-authentic.

approvedCapturePlan.navigationPath: My Workspaces -> Daily Operations -> Links and Assignments

figmaDesignInstructions: Rebuild the operator-control visual from the verified Daily Operations route using assignment readiness, resource linkage, and execution context without exposing phone numbers or other raw PII.

Do not add unsupported operational widgets.

Treat Supplier Balance, Supplier Payments, and Sales Invoices as truth sources only; the currently verified tenant states are too sparse for a strong tuned screenshot.

figmaDesignInstructions: Build a finance-visibility composition faithful to real payables, statements, payment timing, invoice visibility, and status capabilities.
Show supplier balance, invoices, payables, settlement context, and an AI-generated financial or executive report card using safe demo values only.
Use only verified product concepts from Supplier Balance, Supplier Payments, and Invoices; do not invent unsupported finance modules.
Avoid any large embedded text or invented finance modules.

classifications.real_screenshot: Direct product capture with safe demo data and no material visual reconstruction.
classifications.tuned_screenshot: Real product capture with tuned filters, columns, tabs, crop, and demo data to make the screen marketing-ready.
classifications.figma_recreated_mockup: Faithful Figma rebuild or composite based on real Tranzkit workflows when the raw capture is not launch-quality.

rules: Use real screenshots when the real app screen already looks good with demo-safe data.
Tune app state before choosing a Figma rebuild.
Use Figma recreated mockups only when the raw product visual is not marketing-ready.
Do not invent unsupported Tranzkit features, workflows, entities, or data relationships.
Use only demo-safe data and remove any personal, client, location, financial, or token-level sensitive information.
Codefy OS never writes to Sanity. It only prepares media outputs.
Default to shared_language_neutral assets and rely on surrounding CMS copy for language-specific messaging.

releaseMethodRecommendation.note: Recommended for launch, but there is no current approved hero image slot in the content maps.

Prefer a real product capture with tuned state.
Use a faithful rebuild for the current release because the verified Daily Operations state exposed phone numbers and other PII-heavy rows.
Prefer a faithful finance mockup for this release because the verified tenant finance routes are currently too sparse for a strong tuned screenshot.
Switch to a real screenshot if a polished driver-mobile capture becomes available.
Design as a Figma social card.
Design as a Figma social card.

mediaLanguagePolicy.currentRule: One image asset in Sanity, bilingual CMS text around it, and bilingual alt text in the media plan.
defaultDecisionsForThisRelease: Product screenshots and dashboard mockups should be shared language neutral.
Home carousel images should be shared language neutral.
Home feature tab images should be shared language neutral.
Home OG image should be shared language neutral unless a headline is placed inside the image.
Solutions OG image should be shared language neutral unless a headline is placed inside the image.
Avoid large English or Arabic text baked into images.
Use surrounding CMS copy for language-specific messaging.
If a Figma frame needs text, keep it minimal and brand-neutral, such as the Tranzkit logo only.
mediaLanguagePolicy.escalationRule: If an asset becomes locale_specific_required, do not move it to ready_for_transfer until website-side localized image support or an approved workaround exists.
